name: Vampire Survivors
short_description: Mow down thousands of night creatures and survive until dawn! Vampire Survivors is a gothic horror casual game with rogue-lite elements, where your choices can allow you to quickly snowball against the hundreds of monsters that get thrown at you. Be the bullet hell!
detailed_description:
Vampire Survivors is a time survival game with minimalistic gameplay and roguelite elements.
Hell is empty, the devils are here, and there's no place to run or hide. All you can do is survive as long as you can until death inevitably puts an end to your struggles. Gather gold in each run to buy upgrades and help the next survivor.

The supernatural indie phenomenon that lets you be the bullet hell!



Now with local co-op for up to 4 players!

  • Supports mouse, keyboard, controller, and touch screen.

Starting Tips

  • Take your time to grab gems and items, they won't disappear.
  • Get two or three offensive weapons at first, but focus on leveling them up one at a time.
  • Armour and Luck are good starting power-ups to spend money on.
  • Refund power-ups often, it's free, and try new upgrade paths.

v0.1.0 release date:
- itch: Mar 31, 2021
- Steam: Dec 17, 2021

supported_languages: English, French, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain, Japanese, Korean, Polish, Portuguese - Brazil, Russian, Simplified Chinese, Turkish, Ukrainian

reviews.review_text:
I genuinely suspect they put actual crack in this game. My buddy and I got it on game pass as a random way to pass the time on Xbox, then didn't stop playing it for over a month nearly every day. 

It's not borderline addicting, it just IS addicting. 

Rather simplistic in nature, or so you think, there's so much to do and collect that you don't even know where to start, but after a while you look back and realize just how much you've actually done; I started as a mage with a magic wand, and now I'm running towards the King in Yellow to have an easier time fighting a giant skeleton vortex...
Secret hunting hella tuff i ain't gonna lie

Yeah this is pretty great. I played the 'main' game and finished the 'final boss'. Unlocked a lot of content and did some, but not all of the dlc and extra content.

For such a simple game, this game is just plain fun, and I still have so much left to do in it. It's hard to not recommend this for the price it's at.
